title: podophyllum aurantiocaule subsp. furfuraceum
intro: Podophyllum aurantiocaule subsp. furfuraceum, also known as Himalayan mayapple, is a plant species belonging to the barberry family, Berberidaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and anti-cancer properties, which have been attributed to its lignan and phthalide glycosides content. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and exhibiting antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Himalayan mayapple can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
